name: WASM Sandboxed Demo
description: A sandboxed plugin executed through Wasmtime that routes multiple actions (echo, compute, validate) with real WASM logic.

capabilities.description: Returns a response from a sandboxed WebAssembly module.

actions.label: Echo payload
actions.description: Echoes back the request payload with metadata from the WASM sandbox.

actions.label: Compute factorial
actions.description: Computes factorial(10) using iterative multiplication inside the WASM sandbox.

actions.label: Validate payload
actions.description: Validates whether the input payload contains data or is empty/null.

notes: The manifest is authored to remain forward-compatible with additive host/runtime metadata.
The module is intentionally sandboxed and has no outbound I/O.
The module is instantiated per invocation in the current demo runtime.
Hosts may offload invocation to background workers while preserving the same contract.

required.detail: Hosts need to surface the sandboxed response body.

optional.detail: Hosts that understand sandboxed runtimes can label the isolation boundary.
optional.fallback: Fallback hosts can still execute the demo through the default Wasmtime path.

degradation.behavior: If sandboxed-runtime is unavailable the host should omit isolation-specific UI affordances.

notes: Capability negotiation stays local to the host/runtime pair.
This module is authored as WAT to keep the runtime mechanics visible.
A future track can replace it with Rust-authored wasm32 plugins.
